contentHtml:
The great saint Krishnamurti often speaks of the body as a vessel for the divine. She reminds us that our physical form is not a source of shame, but a sacred temple. This is a truth that resonates deep in the marrow of our bones, a truth that the modern world often asks us to forget. We are born into these bodies of bone and blood, and from our first breath, we are taught to see them as separate from our spirit. We are told they are flawed, imperfect, and in need of constant fixing. This is a lie, a poison that seeps into our consciousness and disconnects us from our own divinity. Your body is not a machine to be optimized, nor is it a commodity to be packaged and sold. It is a living, breathing temple, a sacred space where the divine can be known and experienced. ## The Illusion of Separation The illusion of separation is the root of so much of our suffering. We believe we are separate from our bodies, separate from each other, and separate from the divine. This belief is a prison, a cage of our own making. But it is a cage with an open door. We can choose to walk out at any time. In my years of sitting with people from all walks of life, I have seen the profound transformation that occurs when they begin to reclaim their bodies as sacred. A client once described it as coming home to a place she never knew she had left. To dismantle this illusion, we must begin to listen to the wisdom of our bodies. We must learn to trust the signals they send us, the deep knowing that resides in our cells. Your body is a conduit for the divine, a channel for the creative life force of the universe. When you learn to listen to it, you will find that it has been speaking to you all along. *I pray for my eternal alignment with the positive sounds, lights and vibrations that fill all the universes and all the realms.* ## The Altar of the Senses Your senses are the gateways to the divine. They are the portals through which you can experience the beauty, the mystery, and the ecstasy of life. The scent of jasmine on a summer evening, the taste of a ripe mango, the feeling of the sun on your skin – these are not just fleeting pleasures. They are whispers from the divine, invitations to enter into a deeper relationship with the present moment. We must learn to approach our senses with reverence, to see them as altars where we can make offerings of our attention and our presence. The modern world is a constant assault on our senses. We are bombarded with noise, with artificial lights, with processed foods that dull our palates. We are taught to numb ourselves, to disconnect from the raw, untamed energy of our bodies. But your body is a wild thing, a force of nature. It cannot be tamed, but it can be honored. It cannot be controlled, but it can be surrendered to. *I seek to embody the gifts bestowed upon me pre-birth. I seek to release all blocks and attachments that imprison me.* ## The Language of the Body Your body speaks a language that is older than words. It is the language of sensation, of emotion, of intuition. It is a language that is often ignored, dismissed, or pathologized in our culture. We are taught to suppress our anger, to hide our grief, to be ashamed of our desires. But your emotions are not your enemies. They are your allies, your guides, your messengers from the deep. They are the raw, unfiltered expression of your life force. To learn the language of your body, you must be willing to feel. You must be willing to sit with the discomfort, the pain, the messiness of your own humanity. You must be willing to let your heart break open, to let your tears flow, to let your anger burn clean. This is not a path for the faint of heart. It is a path for warriors, for lovers, for those who are willing to walk through the fire of their own being to emerge on the other side, forged in the flames of their own truth. *People who preach their preferences as moral law are not for you.* ## The Sacred Wound We all carry wounds. We are all broken in some way. But our wounds are not a sign of our weakness. They are a sign of our courage, of our resilience, of our capacity to love and to be loved. Your wounds are the places where the light enters you. They are the cracks in the armor of your ego, the openings through which the divine can pour itself into you. Do not be ashamed of your wounds. Do not hide them away. They are a part of your story, a part of your beauty, a part of your power. In the tantric tradition, the wound is seen as a sacred gateway. It is the place where we can meet the divine in its most raw and vulnerable form. It is the place where we can transform our pain into power, our suffering into compassion, our lead into gold. This is the alchemy of the heart, the magic of the soul. This is the path of the wounded healer, the one who has learned to transform their own suffering into a gift for the world. ## The Dance of Polarity Life is a dance of polarities. Light and dark, masculine and feminine, Shiva and Shakti. We are all a part of this dance. We all contain within us the seeds of both creation and destruction, of both love and of rage. The spiritual path is not about transcending these polarities. It is about learning to dance with them, to hold them in a loving embrace, to find the point of balance and harmony between them. *Every time you set a real boundary — sovereign karma. Every time you say 'fuck off' when necessary — self-protective karma.* Your anger is not a sin. It is a sacred fire, a force of nature that can be used to burn away all that is false, all that is holding you back from your own liberation. Your desire is not a weakness. It is a sacred river, a current of life force that can carry you to the ocean of divine love. Do not be afraid of your own power. Do not be afraid of your own wildness. It is your birthright. It is your gift to the world. ## The Temple of Bone and Blood Your body is the temple of bone and blood. It is the place where heaven and earth meet, where spirit and matter become one. It is the altar upon which you can offer your love, your devotion, your very life. Treat it as such. Honor it. Cherish it. Adorn it. It is the most precious gift you have been given. It is the vessel through which you can experience the infinite, the eternal, the divine. Come home to your body. Come home to your senses. Come home to your heart. Come home to the temple of bone and blood. The doors are open. The altar is waiting. The divine is calling your name.

Question 1 about the body temple?

This is the answer to question 1. We must learn to see our bodies not as objects, but as living, breathing expressions of the divine. This is a radical act in a world that teaches us to be ashamed of our physical form.

My dear friend, you are an embodiment of light and love — you are loved for all time — nothing true and pure restricts you. May you remember, on the hard days, that you are the embodiment of infinite love. Your softness is your power. The world will try to harden you. Don't let it.
